name: Jeju (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: the fertile slopes of Hallasan and the abundant nature of the island

description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised multicolour emblem combining curving green, blue and orange forms, with the city brand wordmark 'Only Jeju' below.
history: Jeju, Korea's largest island, has been a Special Self-Governing Province (Teukbyeol-Jachido) since 1 July 2006. The current provincial flag with the 'Only Jeju' brand emblem was introduced as part of the unique self-governing status. The design abstracts Hallasan (the central volcano), the surrounding sea, and the island's signature mandarin oranges.

features.role: Jeju provincial emblem and 'Only Jeju' wordmark
features.symbolism: abstract motif representing Hallasan (the volcanic peak at the centre of the island), the surrounding sea, and Jeju's mandarin oranges; the wordmark 'Only Jeju' expresses the island's distinct cultural and natural identity
